item.title: CRH digs deep for a bigger share of the US construction boom
item.description: Irish building materials group’s deal for Arcosa is worth doing

item.title: EasyJet should hold out for a tad more from Castlelake
item.description: UK budget airline has minimal debt and is expected to grow operating profit sevenfold from this year’s depressed level

item.title: Indian entrepreneur to lead WhatsApp as Zuckerberg brings in new blood
item.description: Fintech founder Kunal Shah to replace Will Cathcart who steps down after seven years leading the Meta-owned messaging app

item.title: EasyJet and its investors hold out for £600mn more from Castlelake
item.description: Shareholders call for private credit group’s £4.7bn approach to be increased to at least £7-a-share

item.title: Chevron moves into power production with Microsoft AI deal
item.description: Company signs 20-year agreement to develop data centre in heart of US oil country that could include gas-fired plant

item.title: AI-powered threats may succeed ‘within months’, Five Eyes warns
item.description: Western governments and corporates cautioned that their lead in AI might not last long

item.title: Building materials group CRH agrees historic $8.5bn deal for Arcosa
item.description: Irish company to purchase Dallas-based construction group

item.title: AbbVie buys Apogee for $10.9bn to acquire inflammatory disease drug
item.description: Drugmaker’s largest deal in more than half a decade comes amid surge of M&A in sector

item.title: Andrew Cuomo to lead new venture between NYSE parent and crypto exchange OKX
item.description: Former New York governor’s role highlights how politicians are rushing into digital assets sector

item.title: ‘Salt is the new oil’
item.description: Better batteries, or Na?

item.title: Over 400 ships are waiting near Hormuz for the strait to reopen fully
item.description: Many tanker operators reluctant to move vessels until Iran and the US strengthen ceasefire deal

item.title: AI law firm wins UK court case for first time
item.description: Freelancer paid about £400 for technology to draft documents for £7,000 claim

item.title: Simon Johnson: ‘Nobody needs as many white-collar workers as they used to’
item.description: The Nobel laureate and former IMF chief economist on how to prepare for what AI will do to jobs

item.title: Germany reaches deal to buy 40% of Europe’s biggest tank maker
item.description: After months of wrangling over KNDS stake, Berlin’s agreement paves the way for an IPO

item.title: BoE dilutes stablecoin rules with plan for £40bn issuer limit
item.description: Proposed regulations would no longer restrict holdings by individuals and companies

item.title: China’s CATL to offer battery swapping for Europe’s electric trucks
item.description: Partnership with Octopus Energy to launch more than 30 stations for electric lorries in Europe by 2035

item.title: Ocado hunts for new chief to replace Tim Steiner
item.description: UK grocery technology group has been hit by international licensing partners scaling back agreements

item.title: The bank supervisor over your shoulder
item.description: I’ve got my eye on you, laddie

item.title: EasyJet rejects £4.7bn takeover bid from Castlelake
item.description: US private credit group goes public with latest 625p-a-share offer after airline’s board refuses to engage

item.title: Robots will replace 700,000 delivery workers ‘sooner or later’, warns JD.com boss
item.description: China’s rapid adoption of technology threatens millions of gig-economy jobs, policymakers fear

item.title: China restricts trading with some US rare earth companies
item.description: Beijing introduces more export controls in retaliation for Washington’s ‘wrongful’ actions

item.title: Do US insurers arbitrage capital regulations with private ratings?
item.description: Probably!

item.title: FirstFT: Starmer’s exit plans expected
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Middle East conflict and Bain’s vibecoding

item.title: Italy open to Germany joining fighter jet project, says Leonardo chief
item.description: Lorenzo Mariani says Berlin role in GCAP would slow progress but bring benefits after collapse of rival FCAS programme

item.title: UK’s out-of-town retail parks ‘effectively full’
item.description: Industry says shortage of space reflects rising demand from some retailers and a dearth of new sites

item.title: Iran war supercharges electric vehicle uptake in Africa
item.description: Boom in demand for electric motorbikes and buses on continent is a boost for China

item.title: New home builds set to fall short of government’s 1.5mn target, Savills says
item.description: Real estate group’s forecast adds to questions over housebuilding agenda in England

item.title: What would a stablecoin run look like?
item.description: Fire sales of assets held in reserve could trigger problems in many other financial markets

item.title: Luxury’s race to secure Milan’s hidden palazzos
item.description: In a bid for the next level of exclusivity, brands pay tens of thousands of euros a day to stage events in the city’s historic residences

item.title: How tennis agents spot the next stars
item.description: Identifying talent rarely follows an exact formula, but building up a network of contacts still acts as the best guide
